product.name: Brokk
product.essence: The forge where work becomes software while you do something else.

mission: An operator glances at one board and knows, in two seconds, what the forge is doing right now — and can hand it the next task in one line.

metaphor.name: The Forge at Night
metaphor.statement: A smithy under the cold mountain sky: an aurora drifts over the peak, the workshop is dark, ordered and quiet — and the only warmth in the frame is the ember of work actually in the fire. Repos are doors along the wall; the queue is the smith's ledger; a running task is metal glowing on the anvil.
metaphor.world: CCL = a mountain under a cold sky (navy + ice-blue); Brokk = the dwarven smith who forged Mjölnir while betting his own head

personality.character: a taciturn master smith — speaks little, shows the work

principles: Numbers over adjectives: '2 running', never 'lots of activity'.
Verb-first imperatives for actions; three words or fewer.
No exclamation marks, no emoji in product copy — the ember is the excitement.
The work is the message: state what happened, name the artifact (PR #, branch, log), stop.
Forge vocabulary is seasoning, not sauce: at most one metaphor word per string, and only where it aids meaning.

action.rule: Verb first, object second, nothing else. The primary action of a surface appears exactly once.
action.example: Queue task · Connect a repo · Open PR
action.counterexample: Click here to create your new task!

emptyState.rule: The cold hearth: zero is the system at rest, never a failure. Name the state in the metaphor, one calm line on what will appear, one clear action to light it.
emptyState.example: The forge is quiet. Queued and running tasks line up here, next-up first. Describe a task above to light it.

error.rule: A smith names what cracked and how to reforge it: the failing step, the artifact to inspect, the next move. No apology theater, no blame on the user.
error.example: Build failed at pnpm install. Read the log, fix, and re-queue — the branch is untouched.
error.counterexample: Oops! Something went wrong. Please try again later.

success.rule: Quiet confirmation; the artifact IS the message. Link it, don't celebrate it.
success.counterexample: 🎉 Success! Your task was completed successfully!

loading.rule: Present-tense verb of the actual step, one word where possible. Progress is shown by the ember, not by prose.
loading.counterexample: Please wait while we process your request…

confirm.rule: State the irreversible consequence flatly, then ask with the verb. Destructive confirmations repeat the object's name.
confirm.example: This deletes forge-b and its queue. Delete forge-b?
confirm.counterexample: Are you sure you want to do this?

display.carries: mastheads (forge-head/fleet titles), vital numerals, section headings, empty-state titles, the wordmark — condensed industrial caps, stamped steel

typography.data: JetBrains Mono — eyebrows, table heads, tile labels, row metadata, sidebar group labels, footer
rules: The display face is ALWAYS uppercase with positive tracking; never used for body copy or controls.
Numerals the operator reads from across the room (vitals) are display-face at ≥2.5rem; everything else stays body/data scale.
Mono marks machine truth: IDs, counts, timestamps, column heads. If a human wrote it, it is not mono.

semantics.--accent: cold structure — links, focus, live/info metrics, active nav
semantics.--line / --line-soft: the only borders — every divider and card edge
semantics.--panel / --panel-2: card surface / hover tier; glass = color-mix(panel 72%, transparent) for floating controls

literal.name: --ember
literal.means: work in the fire — the single warm register in a cold world; v2 renders it as a heat ramp (--ember → --gold) so live work GLOWS instead of dotting
literal.reservation: running work ONLY: forge pulse, running rails/rows/numerals, banked coals in empty hearths, the eyebrow tick. Never decorative fields, never emphasis, never errors.

animations.name: fleet-aurora-drift
animations.means: the sky over the peak is alive

animations.name: fleet-rise
animations.means: content settles into place on load

animations.name: fleet-beam
animations.means: live work sweeps across the rail

motion.reducedMotion: all animations set to none, gradients pinned static — the board reads identically, just still

order.why: identity + the single most important fact: is anything in the fire right now
the fleet's vital signs — running / queued / in review / seats
the primary action — queue work — always one reach away, before inventory
the doors along the wall; live work flagged in ember
the smith's ledger — next-up work across the fleet

signature.name: the ember pulse
signature.what: the one warm heartbeat: 'forging now · N in the fire' chip, molten rails and burning numerals on anything actually executing — heat ramp ember→gold, flipping to a quiet grey when the forge rests

signature.name: the night floor
signature.what: forge-light rises from below the frame, a cold aurora sits over the top edge, film grain makes surfaces material — empty space reads as night air, not dead pixels

signature.name: the stamped nameplate
signature.what: every surface opens with a mono eyebrow carrying a hot tick, then the name struck huge in condensed caps — the operator always knows which room of the smithy they are in

signature.name: the vitals strip
signature.what: one forged bar divided by hairlines, count-up numerals big enough to read from across the room; the running numeral burns ember, other live numerals glow accent

signature.name: the cold hearth
signature.what: empty states as a banked forge — the coals still faintly warm in the mark, one calm line, one action to light it; zero is rest, never failure
